name: Command Execution
description: Command Execution involves monitoring and capturing the execution of textual commands (including shell commands, cmdlets, and scripts) within an operating system or application. These commands may include arguments or parameters and are typically executed through interpreters such as `cmd.exe`, `bash`, `zsh`, `PowerShell`, or programmatic execution.

powershell.description: Retrieves processes running on a system.

linux_shell.description: Retrieves content from a remote URL.

container_environments.description: Executes a command inside a running container.

macos_terminal.description: Lists all users on the system.

events.description: Tracks process creation, including command-line arguments.

mechanisms: Cloud-based log aggregation (AWS CloudWatch, Azure Monitor)

events.description: Logs process creation with command-line arguments.

advanced_logging.description: Monitor command executions via Endpoint Detection and Response solutions.
